up [phrasal verb]1a: to be added together and equal the expected or correct total加起來（得到預期數目）— usually used in negative constructions通常用於否定結構The numbers just don't add up. [=they don't equal the expected total]這些數字加得不對。b: to make sense : to seem to be logical or true說得通；合乎情理They must be lying; their story just doesn't add up.他們肯定在撒謊，他們的說法根本不合情理。c: to slowly increase and become a large number or amount累積；慢慢增多A cup of coffee may only cost a dollar, but those dollars add up quickly.一杯咖啡或許只花一美元，但這一美元很快就會積少成多。I'm saving a little money each month. Eventually it'll add up.我每個月都存點錢。最終這筆錢會積少成多的。The hours that I spend traveling to work really add up.我花在上班路上的時間加起來真不少。2add (something) up or add up (something) : to put together or count (the number or amount of something) to find the total加起來；計算總額Start by adding up how much money you have, and then add up your costs.先算算你有多少錢，再算算要花多少錢。Let's add up all of the money we saved.我們來算一下總共存了多少錢。When you add it all up, he's spent over half of his life in jail.算起來他大半輩子是在監獄裡度過的。3add up to (something)a: to have (a number) as a total合計達；總計達The amount of money she spends on coffee adds up to $2.75 a day.她每天喝咖啡的花費加起來有2.75美元。That adds up to more than a thousand dollars a year.那樣加起來每年會超過一千美元。b: to produce (a specified result)產生（特定結果）These plans should add up to a very exciting vacatio
按照這些計劃應該能夠度過一個非常好玩的假期了。With these three bands playing together, it all adds up to one of the year's best rock concerts.有這三支樂隊同臺獻藝，這將成為今年最好的搖滾音樂會之一。All of the team's hard work didn't add up to a wi

overSee overDefinition of over in English: over preposition ˈəʊvəˈoʊvər1Extending directly upwards from. I saw flames over Berlin cook the sauce over a moderate heat Example sentencesExamples Cook over a high heat until just wilted, cool a bit, then squeeze out most of the moisture. Put the yolk in a metal bowl, held close to a flame but not over it, and mix until it turns white. Melt the fat in a pan, beat the egg and milk together, add to the fat in the pan, season well and cook over a gentle heat. Let it cook over a relatively low heat till the bottom has formed a golden crust. They were meant to be cooked the rest of the way by the diners over a flame that was brought in for that purpose. For a more reliable source, you need to be directly over the clay seam where the fossils are buried. If you position your eyes directly over the ball then your eyes will always be aligned with the hole. Cook over a gentle heat for five minutes, and then add the chopped anchovies and the olives. This occurred when a new building was built directly over the top of the remains of its predecessor. This brings the centre of gravity directly over the right foot, which supports her weight. In a large heavy pan, melt half the butter with the oil over a moderate heat. Soaring directly over our heads, she disappears back into the main current and is gone. We are welcomed in with open arms and hearts, and they prepare beautiful food, cooked over an open fire. Lift the fish out onto warm plates and place the baking dish over a moderate flame. Add olives to the pan and cook over a moderate heat until they begin to wrinkle. Heat the oil in a large saucepan, add the leeks and bay leaves, and fry over a high heat until the leeks have some colour. over/under) noun US 1A bet on whether the outcome of an event (usually the combined score of a sports game) will be above or below a number established in advance by the bookmaker. the over-under in hockey gives you less chance of losing Example sentencesExamples I stayed away from the over-and-under on the winning score, which Sherman posted as 4 1/2 - under-par, because I think that's about the right number. He was more interested in crap games, the over-and-under, the horses, and selling sweaters out of the trunk of his car. There is a special index in the book rating ballparks for Total Number of Runs per game - if you're a Totals (Over-Under) bettor, this would be helpful material. In sports betting, there are just a few bets that are considered to be regular bets and the over/under is one of them.1.1 The number (usually the combined score of a sports game) on which bettors place wagers in an over-under bet. oddsmakers had set the over-under at 53, higher than any other game this week figurative what's the over-under on how many fanatics will camp out? Example sentencesExamples What is the over-under on Weaver? Now, if I were a slow writer, then that could present a problem, especially since I review a lot of adventures, and the over-under for adventures is in the neighborhood of eight hours. Is an over-under of 60 out of the question? What is the over-under on Weaver's arrival in the major leagues? As Walters puts it, "The over-under on me was 39 years old, and not many guys took the over." The site has set the over-under on Peyton Manning's completion percentage vs. New England at 67.5 percent. The Over-Under is in the 47-48 range.2A gun having barrels arranged one above the other rather than side by side. if shooting an over-under, have the upper barrel pointing over and the lower barrel under the bird Example sentencesExamples If I ever do display my gun (a very ornate over-under shotgun built for skeet and trap shooting), it's only to adults, and after I've pulled the case from the back of my closet, opened it, and broken the action to make sure its unloaded. It's tough to beat the Red Label over-and-under. Remington's only over-and-under, the Model 332 12 gauge has been enhanced with a "dressed-up" appearance and traditional engraving. Last year, Browning introduced the Citori 525 over-and-under. The over-under guns tend to be at the more expensive end of the spectrum. The first opal claims were pegged out but, as a result of the unbearable heat and the lack of water, work was abandoned within three weeks. It is essential the site is pegged out before the planning committee visits it, to eliminate any confusio

Within a few days claims were pegged out, tents went up and a blacksmith opened up for business. The new kitchen is pegged out and a safety fence is going up tomorrow! It was expected to remain a small town and as a result only twenty-four townblocks were pegged out.OriginLate Middle English: probably of Low German origin; compare with Dutch dialect peg ‘plug, peg’. out [phrasal verb]1rule (someone or something) out or rule out (someone or something) : to no longer consider (someone or something) as a possibility after careful thought or study不予考慮；排除；取消The police ruled them out as suspects when it was proved that they were out of town when the crime was committed.事實證明案發時他們不在鎮上，因此警方排除了他們是嫌犯的可能。There are some diseases your doctor will want to rule out before making a diagnosis.在做出診斷結論之前，醫生先要排除某些疾病的可能性。2rule (something) out or rule out (something) : to make (something) impossible : to prevent (something) from happening阻止；使不可能The bad weather ruled out a picnic.壞天氣使野餐取消了。3rule (someone) out : to remove (someone) from a competition, contest, etc.使出局；淘汰He applied for the job, but his lack of experience quickly ruled him out.他申請了這份工作，但由於缺乏經驗，很快就被淘汰了。(chiefly Brit) She has been ruled out of today's game with a broken thumb.她折斷了拇指，無法參加今天的比賽了。 rule

我的合同很快就要到期了。b: to become used up耗盡；用完The gasoline ran out before we got to Denver.我們還沒到丹佛，汽油就耗盡了。Our money finally ran out. [=our money was all used; we had no more money]我們的錢最終花光了。2run (someone) out or run (someone) out of (something) old-fashioned : to force (someone) to leave a place迫使（某人）離開An angry mob ran him out of tow

憤怒的暴民把他逐出城外。3run out (something) or run (something) out baseball, of a batter : to run hard to first base after hitting (a batted ball)擊球后跑上一壘His manager got angry because he failed to run out a grounder.他擊出滾地球后沒能跑上一壘，教練因此大怒。4run out or run out of (something) : to use up the available supply of (something)用盡（供給）We're low on gas. We'd better stop before we run out.油快不夠了。我們最好先停車，以免燃料耗盡。He ran out of space and had to put his address on the other side of the paper.他把紙的正面寫滿了，只好把地址寫在了背面。You have to work faster. We're running out of time.你必須加緊工作。我們時間不多了。5run out on (someone) informal : to leave (someone you should stay with) : to abandon or desert (someone)離棄，拋棄（某人）She ran out on her husband and children to be with a man old enough to be her father.她拋棄了丈夫和孩子，跟一個老得可以當她父親的男人跑了。 run

Synonymsunsuited, mismatched, ill-matched, poles apart, worlds apart, like day and nightOriginOld English w(e)oruld, from a Germanic compound meaning 'age of man'; related to Dutch wereld and German Welt.The ancient root of world meant ‘age or life of man’. The first part is the same as were- in werewolf (see wolf)—it means ‘man’—and the second part is related to old. The Anglo-Saxons first used world to mean ‘human existence, life on earth’ as opposed to future life in heaven or hell. America was first called the New World in 1555, and Europe, Asia, and Africa the Old World at the end of that century. Olde worlde is a ‘fake’ antiquated spelling for old-fashioned things intended to be quaint and attractive, and dates only from the 1920s. The developing countries of Asia, Africa, and Latin America were initially known as the Third World in the 1950s by French writers who used tiers monde, ‘third world’, to distinguish the developing countries from the capitalist and Communist blocs. The first use in English came in 1963. The best of all worlds or of all possible worlds is from Candide (1759) by the French writer Voltaire. It is a translation of a statement by the ever-optimistic Pangloss, ‘Everything is for the best in the best of all possible worlds’. The character of Pangloss, who remained constantly cheerful despite all the disasters that happened to him and his travelling companions, is a satire on the views of the German philosopher Leibniz, who believed this philosophy. tie up at 1tietie up [phrasal verb]1tie up or tie (something) up or tie up (something) : to become attached or to attach (something) to a fixed object with a string, rope, etc.被拴住；（用線或繩子等）拴住They tied (the boat) up and jumped out.他們把小船拴好後跳上了岸。The ferry ties up at the south slip.渡船在南部停泊處拋錨。2tie (something) up or tie up (something)a: to deal with (something) in order to complete something處理完；完成The project is almost finished, but we still have a few final details to tie up. = We still have to tie up some loose ends.這個工程基本完工了，但我們最後還有一些細節要處理。The writer ties up all the loose ends at the end of the story.在故事結尾處，作者對所有零星內容做了收尾。bUS : to prevent the use or progress of (something)使…受阻He tied up the phone for an hour. [=he used the phone for an hour and other people could not use it]他佔用了一小時的電話。Traffic was tied up [=backed up] for hours/miles.交通堵了好幾小時/英里。3tie up (money) in (something) or tie (money) up in (something) : to invest (money) in (something) in a way that prevents it from being used for some other purpose佔用，擱死（資金）；使（資金）不能挪用They tied up all of their money in their new business.他們把所有錢都投在了新的生意裡。— usually used as (be) tied up in通常用作(be) tied up inThe money was tied up in stocks.錢被投在股市裡了。4tie up with (something) or be tied up with (something) : to be connected or related to (something)與…有聯絡；與…有關Today's lesson tied up with what was taught yesterday.今天的課和昨天講的有關聯。My life is tied up with hers.我和她的生活密不可分。5tie (someone) up or tie up (someone)a: to tie rope, tape, etc., around the body, arms, or legs of (someone) in order to keep that person from moving or escaping（用繩子、膠帶等）捆綁（某人）The robbers tied up the clerk.劫匪把那名職員綁了起來。b: to prevent (someone) from doing other things or from going to a particular place阻止（某人）做某事；阻止（某人）去某地；使脫不開身Meetings tied me up for most of the afternoo
我一下午大部分時間都在開會。— usually used as (be) tied up通常用作(be) tied upShe was tied up in traffic.交通堵塞，她被堵在路上了。I'd like to help but I'm a bit tied up at the moment.我很願意幫忙，但是目前我脫不開身。—see

washout1 ENTRIES FOUND:washout (noun)washout/ˈwɑːʃˌaʊt/nounplural washoutswashout/ˈwɑːʃˌaʊt/ nounplural washoutsLearner's definition of WASHOUT[count] informal1: a complete failure徹底失敗；失敗者He was a washout as a professional golfer.作為一名職業高爾夫球手，他是失敗者。The team lost so many games that the season was a total washout.這個隊輸掉了那麼多場比賽，以至於本賽季徹底失敗。2: an event, game, etc., that is canceled because of rain因下雨而取消的事Yesterday's game was a washout.昨天的比賽因下雨取消了。—see

wash out at 1wash wash out [phrasal verb]1: to be able to be removed or cleaned by washing能被洗掉The wine stain won't wash out.這塊酒漬洗不掉了。2wash (something) out or wash out (something)a: to clean the inside of (something, such as a cup or pot) with water洗淨，清洗（杯子、壺等內部）Just wash out the coffee cups before you go.你把那些咖啡杯都洗乾淨後再走。b: to damage or carry away (something) by the force of moving water沖垮；沖毀；沖走The flooding river washed out the bridge.氾濫的河水把那座橋沖垮了。The flood washed out the road.洪水沖垮了路面。c: to cause (something, such as a sports event) to be stopped or canceled because of rain（因下雨而）停止，取消（體育比賽等）Rain washed out Friday's game.大雨使得週五的比賽取消了。This weekend's game was washed out.這週末的比賽因為下雨而被取消。3US, informal : to fail to successfully complete a course of training because you do not have the necessary qualities, skills, or abilities未能完成培訓專案；出局This program is so tough, at least 30 percent of the students will wash out before the end of the first year.這個課程太難了，第一學年結束前至少有30%的學生將無法達標。—see
